title: Cambridge: King's College May Ball (William & Bryony)

main_route.excerpt: Samon + Eve set off at 4 for Cambridge + parked at William's house, 7 Maddingley Rd.

main_route.inference_reason: All trips end at home unless diary states otherwise; no return narrative recorded (mixed_end page)

stops.excerpt: Friends fetched William + Bryony from Kings College, where they had been getting ready for the May Ball. William had managed to get us first-sitting dinner tickets in the long room at 8 pm.

boundary_flags: mixed_end: page A_0342_L is shared with the following trip; no return journey recorded
summary: Simon and Eve drove from Birmingham to Cambridge on 17 June 2003 for the King's College May Ball, joining William and his partner Bryony. They enjoyed first-sitting dinner in the famous candlelit Long Room at King's College, followed by fireworks.
extraction_notes: Only one diary page survives for this trip (A_0342_L, mixed_end=true). The date 'Tuesday June 17th' resolves to 2003-06-17 consistent with the trip_id year. The iso field in the source JSON incorrectly shows 1997-06-17 — the correct year is 2003. Return to Birmingham is inferred per standard convention; no return narrative is present. William is listed as a traveller in the title context but was already at Cambridge (a student); the travelling party from Birmingham comprised Simon and Eve only. King's College is treated as an excursion from the Cambridge overnight base rather than a separate main-route stop.
